questions.question: In distributed-memory parallelism, how do processes exchange data?
options: By reading and writing shared global memory protected by mutexes
By sending explicit messages through a network
By using atomic compare-and-swap on a shared cache line
By spawning threads that inherit the parent's address space
questions.explanation: Distributed-memory systems have no shared address space. Processes must communicate by sending messages (e.g., via MPI_Send/MPI_Recv). Shared memory with mutexes or atomics is the shared-memory model.

questions.question: What does MPI_COMM_WORLD represent?
options: A communicator containing only processes running on the same physical machine
A variable that stores the number of MPI processes in the job
The default communicator that includes every process launched by mpirun
A function that returns the rank of the calling process
questions.explanation: MPI_COMM_WORLD is the default communicator created by MPI_Init. It contains all processes launched by mpirun/mpiexec. Use MPI_Comm_rank to query a process's ID within it.

questions.question: You write an MPI program where every rank calls MPI_Send before MPI_Recv in a ring. The program hangs. What is the most likely cause?
options: MPI_Init was called after MPI_Send
The communicator is too small
A deadlock caused by every process blocking on Send waiting for a matching Recv
The message tag values are out of order
questions.explanation: If every process calls MPI_Send (blocking) before any calls MPI_Recv, a synchronous send waits for a matching receive that never arrives — classic deadlock. Fix: reorder so at least one process posts a Recv first, or use MPI_Sendrecv / non-blocking calls.

questions.question: In a Jacobi 2-D solver with row-based domain decomposition, what is the purpose of halo exchange?
options: To reduce the total number of floating-point operations per iteration
To communicate boundary rows between neighbouring sub-domains so interior stencil updates are correct
To sum the residual across all processes after each iteration
To evenly divide the grid columns among all processes
questions.explanation: Each process owns a slab of rows. The stencil (u[i-1] + u[i+1] + ... ) needs the row above and below the local slab. Those boundary rows come from neighbouring processes — that is the halo exchange. Collectives like MPI_Allreduce then aggregate the global residual.

questions.question: What is MPI (Message Passing Interface)?
options: A shared memory programming model
A standard for message-passing between processes in distributed memory systems (clusters of computers)
A GPU programming framework
A web protocol for microservices
questions.explanation: MPI is the standard for programming distributed memory clusters. Each process has its own address space and communicates by sending/receiving messages (MPI_Send, MPI_Recv, MPI_Bcast). It enables scaling to thousands of nodes for HPC workloads like weather simulation and molecular dynamics.
